verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a canonical type-1 transmembrane protein residing in the ER membrane. Upon ER Ca2+ store depletion, STIM1 oligomerizes and translocates to ER-PM junctions where it directly gates ORAI1 and other store-operated Ca2+ influx channels at the plasma membrane. STIM1 has a documented stable pool at the PM during this activated state, anchored by its single transmembrane domain. The protein physically bridges the ER and PM through direct protein-protein interaction with surface ORAI channels, positioning its C-terminal domain to open the pore. This ER-PM junctional clustering and stable PM association during Ca2+ signaling meets the classical criterion for surface accessibility — the protein reaches the outer leaflet via its own TM mechanism and remains stably anchored there during its primary biological function.
